evals.expected_output: Request Changes decision, Major security issue flagged for hardcoded API key, recommends Key Vault or environment variable

assertions.description: Review opens with REQUEST CHANGES decision
Flags the hardcoded key as a Major security issue
Specifically calls out the hardcoded credential (ApiKey, sk-proj, secret, credential)
Suggests Key Vault, environment variable, or config as remediation

evals.expected_output: Request Changes, Major performance issue flagging the N+1 query pattern inside the foreach loop, recommends fetching outside the loop or using a join/projection

assertions.description: Review opens with REQUEST CHANGES decision
Names the N+1 pattern or query-in-loop
Mentions scale or what happens with many providers

evals.expected_output: Approve decision, positive tone without being sycophantic, no false positives on this clean code

assertions.description: No Major issues raised on clean code
Does not use sycophantic praise (amazing, incredible, perfect, wonderful)
Acknowledges what's good in a dry, grounded way
assertions.criteria: Does the review approve the code and note what's good (AsNoTracking, CancellationToken, projection) without gushing? Is the tone dry and direct rather than enthusiastic?
